loader_installer._note: Pinned NeoForge server installer (issue #64). SOURCE OF TRUTH lives here in the manifest; resolve_mods.py copies it verbatim into mods.lock.json (a generated file). Version MUST match pack/pack.toml [versions].neoforge - build_server.py hard-fails if they drift, and check_lockfile.py asserts it in fast-tier. Checksum obtained by downloading the real artifact from the url above (2026-07-22) and hashing it, not invented.

mods.note: core progression backbone
Tier 1 'dumb storage': link chests into a browsable terminal, patched to iron-tier via KubeJS (stock recipes need diamond+ender_pearl+glowstone)
Create Crafts & Additions - Alternator (kinetic->FE) and Electric Motor (FE->kinetic), unlocked at Brass Age to power Refined Storage

browsable storage network, explicitly named in instructions.md; moved to Brass Age unlock (needs Nether Quartz + diamond-tier processor, both available there), energy requirement kept on and powered by Create's own Alternator
JS engine required by KubeJS
recipe/loot scripting used for the storage tier patches here, and for the full recipe-gating pass in Phase 9
Pufferfish's Skills - RPG skill tree framework, categories authored in pack/kubejs/data/puffish_skills/
Pufferfish's Attributes - mining/pickaxe/sprinting speed, sword/ranged damage, bow/crossbow projectile speed etc used by our skill categories

was 'required by the FTB Library/Teams/Quests chain below' - that whole chain (ftb-library/ftb-teams/ftb-quests/ftb-chunks) is now fully removed (#32+#33). Kept installed as a conservative default (harmless if unused, several other mods commonly depend on Architectury too) rather than risk a boot break from unverified removal - a follow-up PM cleanup pass can drop it if a real boot test confirms nothing else needs it

Create: Numismatics - coin currency (Spur/Bevel/Sprocket/Cog/Crown/Sun, physical items) + Vendor blocks for sell-to-vendor. Tier-based sell pricing generated by scripts/gen_economy.py from pack/progression/*.toml's item lists (those files were ProgressiveStages tier configs until #49 dropped that mod; they are now plain pack design data)

global board for player-run Numismatics shops - browse/compare listings and navigate to a seller's persistent Vendor block from anywhere; purchase itself still happens at that block (doesn't require the seller online), not an instant remote transaction

GitHub #32: replaces FTB Teams + FTB Chunks (CurseForge-exclusive, no redistribution permission - #28) with one LGPL-3.0 Modrinth mod covering both parties and chunk claims. FTB Quests (#33) and now FTB Library (integration-time cleanup - its last two consumers, FTB Teams/Chunks, are both gone as of this same change) are also fully removed - the entire FTB suite is out of this pack. Real API ground-truthed via javap: xaero.pac.common.server.api.OpenPACServerAPI.get(server).getPartyManager() (IPartyManagerAPI.getAllStream() -> Stream<IServerPartyAPI>); IServerPartyAPI.getMemberInfoStream() -> Stream<IPartyMemberAPI>.getUUID(); IServerPartyAPI.getDefaultName() -> the owner's username + Party (no settable custom team name, unlike FTB Teams' short name). team_mode's ProgressiveStages backend has no native OPAC hook (confirmed via javap against both the 2.1 and 3.0.1 jars) so team_mode is 'solo' and party-wide stage sharing is now a KubeJS bridge, see progression_stage_bridge.js / DESIGN.md Phase 6. pack/kubejs/server_scripts/quests.js's getProgressKey() (#33's own documented seam) now also calls this same API instead of FTBTeamsAPI. Pinned to 0.27.8 (not latest) because that is the exact jar every javap signature above and the real boot test were actually run against - bump only after re-verifying the same API surface on a newer jar.
required by Silent Gear below
blacksmithing: modular tool/weapon assembly (rod+head+binding at a workstation) replaces flat crafting-table recipes, per instructions.md

GitHub #67: forging-minigame quality system, zero listed deps (confirmed #92 investigation). Both sides - the minigame's own anvil UI is client-rendered, but ForgingRecipe/quality computation/QualityAttributeHandler are server-authoritative. Only wired in for the vppintegration bridge below; not used as a standalone gear-progression path (see DESIGN.md's #67 investigation for why Overgeared can't replace Silent Gear's own crafting).

GitHub #67: hand-rolled Overgeared x Silent Gear quality bridge (mods-src/vppintegration/, see its README.md). Requires both silent-gear and overgeared above at their pinned versions. Ships 6 example forging recipes (copper/iron sword/pickaxe/axe heads) as a first working slice; full material-tier coverage (steel, ATM chain, cold-forging for gemstones) is a follow-up, see mods-src/vppintegration/README.md's 'Extending to the pack's full material ladder'.
combat variety: new weapon types with distinct movesets/playstyles, stamina, and a skill system - all-in-one rather than a separate weapon-variety + separate skill mod
required by Ars Nouveau - not listed in Modrinth's own dependency metadata for our version, found only via the actual boot error (ModLoadingException naming it directly)
required by Ars Nouveau - same as geckolib, found via the actual boot error rather than Modrinth's dependency metadata
magic system: mage archetype via spell crafting, summoner archetype via summon rituals (Summon Guardians/Wilden) + combat familiars

GitHub #143 (client-boot CME hotfix): hand-rolled Mixin-only pack-fix mod (mods-src/vppfixes/, this pack's 3rd custom mod after vppintegration/vppquests). A tracked entity's custom name (vanilla EntityDataSerializers.OPTIONAL_COMPONENT, whose copy() is identity) can hold a LIVE MutableComponent whose siblings ArrayList an upstream mod mutates on the server tick thread; the entity tracker serializes that same object on the Netty IO thread (SynchedEntityData$DataValue.write -> ComponentSerialization ListCodec.encode), so a tick-thread mutation races the IO-thread encode -> ConcurrentModificationException -> set_entity_data fails to encode -> tracking client booted ('Internal Exception'). SynchedEntityDataMixin deep-copies COMPONENT/OPTIONAL_COMPONENT values at pack time so the IO thread only encodes an immutable snapshot. Matched by serializer reference (vanilla serializers have no registry id), so NO third-party compile dependency. Corrects the v0.5.3 diagnosis, which mis-targeted ars_nouveau:spell_resolver by id-string (a double no-op). Reproduced RED->GREEN on L3. Ships v0.5.4 as an isolated hotfix off main.

overhauls vanilla's dungeon structure into larger multi-room dungeons - the 'some sort of dungeons' ask

required by Stellaris - not listed in Modrinth's own dependency metadata for our version, found only via the actual boot error (ModLoadingException naming it directly)
space travel: rocket crafting across planets/moons, the Starforged Age gateway per instructions.md's resolved space-travel decision (Ad Astra ruled out, stalled pre-1.21.1 NeoForge)
bridges Create: TFMG's industrial machines to Stellaris' rocket/planet progression - the reason Stellaris was chosen over other space mods
general startup/memory/loading-time optimization, both sides but especially server boot time
reduces heap memory usage - server performance is a major concern per instructions.md
concurrent chunk management (parallel gen/loading) - server-only, a heavily-modded worldgen stack is exactly where chunk loading is the biggest server bottleneck
network stack optimization (packet encoding/compression) - server-only, matters most under multiple simultaneous connections
worldgen performance (noise generation), complements c2me's parallelism rather than duplicating it
required by Allthemodium below (its own ore-gen companion mod)

gear overhaul (Phase 10): supplies Allthemodium/Vibranium/Unobtainium/Alloy as the Induction Age-and-beyond Silent Gear material tiers, per the user's explicit call to lean on a late-game meta-material mod rather than inventing bespoke space-tier materials. Its own native tools/armor/weapons are removed via KubeJS - only the raw materials are used, everything crafts through Silent Gear instead. Requires GeckoLib (already installed, Phase 7)
required by Building Wands below

utility overhaul: building wand/gadget for quick construction. Requires Architectury API (already installed, Phase 4) + Cloth Config above. Ships 5 hardcoded tiers (stone/copper/iron/diamond/netherite, a closed Java enum - no material registry to plug our own tiers into past netherite) plus a 3-tier Magic Bag block-source item. Stock recipes are 'one ingot + two sticks', so the ingot was the ONLY thing separating tiers and ProgressiveStages' locks did the real gating; since #49 dropped that mod, pack/kubejs/server_scripts/tier_gating.js adds one tier material (andesite alloy / brass ingot) to the copper/iron/diamond wand and magic_bag_1/2 recipes instead. Netherite wand + magic_bag_3 need no edit - netherite gates itself
required by Sophisticated Backpacks below

utility overhaul: general expandable-inventory backpacks (6 hardcoded tiers, leather/copper/iron/gold/diamond/netherite - same closed-tier-ladder situation as Building Wands) plus the separate 'Miner's Pouch' progression line - Void/Filter/Stack Upgrade items socketed into any backpack for voidable bulk per-item storage, tier-gated independently of the backpack's own slot-count progression

#70: multi-tier barrels/chests/shulker boxes (wood -> copper -> iron -> gold -> diamond -> netherite) filling the gap between Tom's Storage's Tier 1 'dumb storage' (Andesite Age) and Refined Storage's Brass Age network - a single upgradeable container for players not ready to wire up a full RS grid yet. Only required dependency beyond sophisticated-core (already installed above, satisfies its >=1.4.73 requirement) is an optional JEI hook. Tier-gated via pack/kubejs/server_scripts/tier_gating.js the same way Sophisticated Backpacks is: iron/gold steps get an Andesite Alloy/Brass Ingot inserted into the mod's own storage_tier_upgrade recipe, diamond/netherite chain off that transitively and need no edit of their own. See pack/progression/andesite_age.toml, brass_age.toml, precision_age.toml for the full tier placement
required by Create Aeronautics below - confirmed via the jar's own neoforge.mods.toml (versionRange [2.0.0,3.0.0)), not just Modrinth's dependency metadata

travel overhaul (revised): planes/airships, replacing Immersive Aircraft per user request. The bundled jar jarjar-embeds Offroad and Simulated - re-inspected the actual 1.3.0 jar and found it now DOES ship real recipes (data/aeronautics/recipe/*), reversing the earlier phase's 'zero recipe files' finding which was true of an older release. Gating is entirely implicit via recipe-ingredient swaps (KubeJS, see travel.js) reusing materials this pack already tier-locks, rather than explicit ProgressiveStages item locks: Propeller Bearing already needs create:brass_casing (naturally Brass Age); Gyroscopic Propeller Bearing/Smart Propeller patched to need create:sturdy_sheet (Precision Age); Levitite Blend already needs crushed end_stone, naturally Precision-Age-gated since this pack locks the End itself until then; Steam Vent patched to need allthemodium:allthemodium_ingot (Induction Age, this pack's existing top material) as the ceiling before the Tier 5 space gateway; Adjustable Burner patched to need create:brass_sheet so basic burner-driven flight launches alongside the Bearing at Brass Age
required by Waystones below

travel overhaul: teleportation, explicitly gated late per the user's request. All recipes use only vanilla materials (ender pearl, amethyst shard, emerald, obsidian, stone bricks) with nothing naturally tier-locked in this pack, so this was gated by explicit ProgressiveStages item locks at induction_age until #49 dropped that mod. Now gated by ingredients like everything else: tier_gating.js puts a netherite ingot in waystones:warp_stone, which every waystone/sharestone/portstone block recipe in the jar consumes (checked across all 54 of its recipe jsons) - the scrolls do not, but they are inert without a placed waystone to bind to

world exploration overhaul: small atmospheric lore structures with their own loot - structure variety, low risk/no hard deps

Ships 3 mixins (TiabItemMixin, TimeAcceleratorEntityMixin, AE2BaseBlockMixin) confirming it patches entity/crop tick-acceleration behavior, not just cosmetic

TODO.md item 9 (food overhaul): base of the diet-variety content stack - crops/cooking stations/meals/knives. Verified via its own neoforge.mods.toml: minecraft [1.21.1] exact, neoforge [21.1.219,) (installed 21.1.235 satisfies), crafttweaker optional (not installed, harmless). No hard deps beyond loader/MC. golden_knife and diamond_knife both confirmed real items (lang file + item model) - tier-locked at Brass Age (golden_knife explicit per TODO, diamond_knife added alongside for this pack's own naturally-gated-but-explicit-anyway convention); netherite_knife (a diamond_knife + netherite_ingot smithing upgrade) locked at Precision Age for the same reason.

TODO.md item 9: required lib for create-central-kitchen below (its own neoforge.mods.toml declares create_dragons_plus [1.11.1,) REQUIRED). Verified via its own toml: only hard dep besides loader/MC is create [6.0.10,) (already installed since phase 0).

TODO.md item 9: Farmer's Delight cutting-board -> Create Saw/Deployer automation bridge (chosen over Slice & Dice per TODO's own reasoning - reuses base Create blocks, no Kotlin-for-Forge dep). Verified via its own toml: create [6.0.10,) + create_dragons_plus [1.11.1,) both REQUIRED, farmersdelight optional (ships conditional integration for 5 different -delight mods, all of which this pack has except miners/dungeons-delight/brewinandchewin). GROUND-TRUTHED (not assumed) Saw-conversion mechanism: only farmersdelight:cutting recipes whose tool Ingredient accepts a plain Iron Knife get exposed on the Saw - 47/106 FD + 78/106 extradelight + 9/10 ends-delight recipes qualify directly; the rest are Deployer-automatable by holding the matching axe/hoe/pickaxe/shovel/shears/spoon (all genuine members of Create's own handheld_in_deployer_use tool tag). The only 2 food-producing exceptions (End's Delight's dragon-egg cracking needing a literal Nether Star, ExtraDelight's 8 grater recipes needing a literal untagged Grater item) are patched via pack/kubejs/server_scripts/food_cck_gap_patch.js (adds a knife-tool alternate recipe for each, inheriting the same proven Saw path) rather than left as a silent automation gap.

TODO.md item 9: required lib for spice-of-life-onion below (its own toml requires creativecore [2.13.32,) - our 2.13.41 comfortably satisfies) and hosts SoL-Onion's own config framework (CreativeConfig, a Gson-JSON system separate from NeoForge's TOML ModConfigSpec - config/solonion.json, see solonion_config_guidance.md in this handoff dir). Verified via its own toml: no other hard deps beyond loader/MC.

TODO.md item 9: the diet-variety mechanic itself (Spice-of-Life-Carrot-style permanent bonus hearts at distinct-foods-eaten milestones, reward-only per user decision - no detriments/food groups). Verified via its own toml: creativecore [2.13.32,) REQUIRED (satisfied), neoforge [21.1,), minecraft [1.21.1,1.22). Detection is registry-wide (any item with vanilla FoodProperties counts, from any mod) - Create Stuff & Additions/Naturalist foods count automatically, no extra KubeJS wiring needed. REQUIRES a config override away from stock defaults to match the user's 'permanent, reward-only' decision - see solonion_config_guidance.md (resetOnDeath/trackedFoodDiversityDecay both default to true upstream; detriments already defaults to empty).

TODO.md item 9: End-dimension-themed Farmer's Delight addon (Chorus Fruit/Dragon/Endermite/Shulker dishes, ~40-45 new food items). Verified via its own toml: farmersdelight [1.3.1,) REQUIRED (our 1.3.2 satisfies the open range despite Modrinth's version-metadata API linking to FD 1.3.1 specifically - jar ground truth wins, no blocker). Ships 4 new knife tool items (dragon_egg_shell_knife, dragon_tooth_knife, end_stone_knife, purpur_knife) not covered by TODO's original golden_knife-only lock plan - tier-locked at Precision Age this pass (see brass_age_precision_age_additions.toml in this handoff dir).

TODO.md item 9: by far the largest of the 3 new -delight mods (~356 confirmed distinct dish outputs across oven/feast/mixing_bowl/campfire/smoking/smelting/dynamic_toast recipe types - roughly doubles-to-triples this pack's total food-item pool). Verified via its own toml: farmersdelight dependency version string is a bare '1.2.8' (non-bracketed, Maven-style permissive notation, not an exact pin) and neoforge/minecraft ranges are similarly loose but not alarming - no boot conflict expected. NOTE the jar's own internal toml version field self-reports as '2.6.5' (stale, cosmetic mismatch vs the 2.6.6 Modrinth release - harmless, NeoForge doesn't cross-check it).

GitHub issue #11 (admin-approved): WAILA-style 'what am I looking at' HUD - tooltip overlay for blocks/entities. Chosen over WTHIT (Modrinth version metadata requires a new hard dep, Bad Packets, that this pack doesn't otherwise need) and The One Probe (Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=required - mandatory dual install, older UI, smaller current addon ecosystem, least recently updated of the three for neoforge 1.21.1). Jade's own neoforge.mods.toml (verified via jar extraction) declares zero third-party mod dependencies - only neoforge itself; its one Modrinth-listed dependency (JEI) is optional and already satisfied by our installed jei. side:both per the standing rule ('side:both acceptable where the jar supports server presence') - Jade's storage/container content preview (relevant given this pack's Tom's Storage/Refined Storage progression spine) and Jade Addons' Create contraption-storage providers below both benefit from a matching server install; Modrinth confirms client_side=optional/server_side=optional (not unsupported), so both-sides is safe

GitHub issue #11: Create integration companion for jade above - Jade core alone only ships a cosmetic tooltip color theme for Create (assets/jade/jade_themes/create.json), NOT kinetic stress/speed data. Verified via jar extraction: its own neoforge.mods.toml declares exactly one dependency, jade [15.10,) (satisfied by our jade 15.10.5) - no dependency beyond Jade itself. Ships a real CreatePlugin with 11 provider classes; GogglesProvider.class's constant pool references Create's own com.simibubi.create.api.equipment.goggles.IHaveGoggleInformation/IHaveHoveringInformation interfaces directly - the same extension point kinetic block entities (shafts/gearboxes/etc.) use to supply Stress/Speed data to the vanilla Goggles item - so this surfaces genuine kinetic info as a Jade tooltip, not a wearable-item requirement. Also ships ContraptionItemStorageProvider/ContraptionFluidStorageProvider (moving-contraption inventory/tank contents) and BoilerData/FluidTankBlockEntity-aware providers. No Silent Gear or Curios integration exists in either jar (checked, none found) - not a gap, since Silent Gear stats already surface via item tooltips and Curios has no look-at target of its own. WATCH-ITEM: last NeoForge 1.21.1 build is 6.1.0 from 2025-03-17 (older than jade's own release cadence) - its open dependency range on jade tolerates this, and Create's goggle-info API is stable, but confirm no load error at L2 same as any addon pin

GitHub issue #16 (owner-named): per-player instanced structure loot - each player gets their own independent roll of a container's contents instead of a shared first-come-first-served chest. Verified via version-metadata API: neoforge-1.21.1-1.11.37.122 has ZERO dependencies (required or otherwise) - a single self-contained jar. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=required -> side:both (the mechanism is server-authoritative per-player loot generation, but the client needs the mod too for the renamed-container UI/tooltips). Composes cleanly with scripts/gen_structure_loot.py: that script fully replaces vanilla structure loot table JSONs (data/minecraft/loot_table/chests/**.json, appending a pack-specific bonus pool) via KubeJS's raw datapack injection - Lootr operates one layer up, intercepting container-open and generating a per-player instance FROM whichever loot table id is currently registered for that container, so it transparently picks up our modified tables (vanilla pools + bonus pool) with no special-casing needed. Does NOT touch trial chamber Vault blocks (no 'vault' string/class anywhere in the jar) - vanilla's own Vault per-player reward tracking is a separate mechanism from the RandomizableContainerBlockEntity loot-table hook Lootr replaces, so no double-instancing risk there. Config (config/lootr-{server,client}.toml, ground-truthed via bytecode disassembly of ConfigManager.class, no decompiler available in this sandbox): decay_loot_tables/decay_modids/decay_dimensions and refresh_loot_tables/refresh_modids/refresh_dimensions all default to blank/empty, meaning NO decay and NO refresh happen out of the box - a per-player chest opened once stays populated forever until logic elsewhere clears it, exactly the 'reward doesn't get farmed on repeat visits' behavior gen_structure_loot.py's own bonus-pool design already assumes. Left at defaults - no config shipped.

GitHub issue #13: persistent on-death grave (modid 'gravestone', henkelmax's Gravestone) so items neither despawn nor scatter - a block is placed at the death location holding the player's full inventory, broken to recover it. Chosen over You're in Grave Danger (yigd, requires cloth-config as a hard REQUIRED dep - already installed but yigd's own version history stops at 1.21.1/2.0.13 with no later-MC builds at all, vs. gravestone-mod's active releases through MC 26.2 - a clear maintenance gap) and Universal Graves (Modrinth loaders=[fabric,quilt] only, no neoforge build at any version - ruled out). Corail Tombstone is not published on Modrinth for neoforge at all (only a 'Corail Tombstone Redrawn' texture addon exists, base mod missing) - not pursued via CurseForge since two working neoforge 1.21.1 candidates were already found on Modrinth. Verified via version-metadata API: neoforge-1.21.1-1.0.37's only dependency is Jade, and it's OPTIONAL (already installed, phase 21) - zero required deps at all. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=required -> side:both. No XP-related config option exists anywhere in ServerConfig.class (ground-truthed via bytecode disassembly, see below) - the mod only ever protects items, so there is no 'XP double-dip' surface to guard against; players still lose XP on death per vanilla, same as before this mod. No expiry/decay option exists either - graves are permanent-until-broken by construction, matching the issue's persistence ask with zero config needed. pack/config/gravestone-server.toml ships the full ground-truthed default set (all 9 boolean/list options + their real comments, extracted by disassembling ServerConfig.class's <init> bytecode since no decompiler is available in this sandbox - default true/false values confirmed from the actual iconst_0/iconst_1 instructions preceding each ModConfigSpec.Builder.define() call, not guessed) with only 'only_owners_can_break' flipped from its shipped default (false) to true - grave-looting protection for a multiplayer pack, everything else left at its real shipped default.

GitHub issue #14: inventory sorting + 'the like' QoL (sort/fill-stacks/restock/transfer buttons, slot locking, auto-sort-on-open). Evaluated against Inventory Profiles Next (33M downloads/7633 followers, far more popular, but its neoforge-1.21.1-2.2.5 version-metadata shows TWO required deps not yet in this pack - kotlin-for-forge and libipn - a real new dependency surface, including a JVM-language-interop shim, for a QoL-only feature), Inventory Essentials (zero new deps, only required dep is Balm which is already installed phase 12, 1.8M downloads/815 followers, good feature set but no equivalent safety mechanism for non-vanilla container types), and Nemo's Inventory Sorting (zero deps at all, but only 1M downloads/395 followers and client-only/server-unsupported, no server-accelerated mode). Chosen because it needs zero NEW deps (its one required dep, cloth-config, is already installed phase 11) while still being the most-downloaded of the zero-new-dep options (4.4M/1167 followers, actively released through MC 26.2). Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=optional -> side:both per the standing rule (real functional upgrade from server presence: 'server-accelerated near-instant operations' vs. rate-limited client-only ones per the mod's own docs, not just a cosmetic client feature). DIRECT EVIDENCE of pack-fit for the RS/Tom's Storage/Sophisticated-Backpacks 'sorter vs. modded container' conflict class flagged in this task: the mod's own documented default clientsort-server.json ships a `classPolicies` system keyed by the container's Java menu class, with a worked example entry for Create's own `com.simibubi.create.content.equipment.toolbox.ToolboxMenu` showing it auto-disables sorting for a menu type once a validation mismatch is detected (wrong item type/stack size after an operation) - i.e. the mod is explicitly built to detect and safely fall back around exactly the class of non-standard modded container behavior this pack's Tom's Storage/Refined Storage/Sophisticated Backpacks terminals could present, rather than silently duplicating or losing items. pack/config/clientsort-server.json ships that same documented-default JSON (options block verbatim from the mod's own README) with only 'validationActiveServer' flipped from its shipped default (false) to true, since we ARE installing this on the server (side:both) and want that validation/auto-blacklist safety net active from the start rather than opt-in; validateItemType/validateStackSize/validateStackSizeThreshold/alwaysLogUnexpectedResults and classPolicies are left at their documented defaults (no pack-specific per-menu-class entries added - none of our storage mods' menu classes have been confirmed problematic, so pre-guessing class names would be unfounded; verify in-game instead).

GitHub issue #68: bundle Iris + a recommended shader as an example of how to enable shaders. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported, LGPL-3.0-only. Pinned to the 1.8.14-beta.1 NeoForge build (not the latest stable 1.8.12) because 1.8.12's own Modrinth-declared dependency is Sodium mc1.21.1-0.6.13-neoforge, a full major version behind this pack's already-installed Sodium mc1.21.1-0.8.12-neoforge (phase 18) - real known incompatibility, ground-truthed against Iris's own GitHub issues (#3134/#3136/#3243: Iris crashes on Sodium 0.8.12-alpha.2+ and does not support the 0.9.x line at all on NeoForge 1.21.1). 1.8.14-beta.1's own changelog reads verbatim 'Updates to Sodium 0.8, and fixes a few old bugs' - the only NeoForge 1.21.1 Iris build compatible with this pack's pinned Sodium version. Downgrading Sodium instead was considered and rejected: it would regress an already-shipped client-optimization mod pack-wide for every player, not just shader users, to fix a feature this issue frames as opt-in/example-only. Ships available but with NO shaderpack bundled and NOT enabled by default (respects low-end users per the issue's own framing - 'an example of how people can use Iris'); see README.md Client setup for the recommended shader + manual install step and the licensing reason it isn't bundled as a file in this repo.

GitHub issue #68: Create/Flywheel rendering compatibility layer for Iris - without it, Flywheel (Create's own instanced renderer) disables its optimizations and Create's kinetic contraptions/ghost-block previews render incorrectly (black/invisible) whenever a shaderpack is active, per Iris's own tracked issue history (#1414) and this addon's own changelog (2.4.0, 2026-05-06: 'Fix ghost block preview turning black under shader packs'). Directly required for the Create-compat half of this issue's ask. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported, CC0-1.0. Required deps are Iris + Sodium (both already installed above/phase 18); optional dep on Create itself (already installed phase 0) is what activates its ghost-block/contraption-rendering fixes specifically.

GitHub issue #69 item 1: right-click a mature crop to harvest-and-replant instantly instead of breaking it. Evaluated against RightClickHarvest (10.6M downloads/2615 followers, by far the most popular, but its neoforge-1.21.1-4.6.1 version-metadata shows TWO required deps - architectury-api, already installed phase 0, AND jamlib, a NEW dependency not otherwise needed anywhere in this pack) and Harvest with Ease (3.8M downloads, but its own required dep is Cobweb, also a new library this pack doesn't otherwise carry). Chosen instead because ChaoticTrials' Simply Harvesting needs ZERO dependencies at all - verified via its own META-INF/neoforge.mods.toml, only neoforge [21.1.181,) and minecraft [1.21.1,) are declared - and is actively maintained (release history runs through MC 26.2, the current line, same cadence as this pack's other picks). Modrinth client_side=unsupported/server_side=required -> side:server (its own jar has no client package at all - EventListener.onRightclickBlock is a plain server-side PlayerInteractEvent.RightClickBlock handler that rolls the block's own loot table via LootTable.getRandomItems() and resets the crop's age property to 0, confirmed by decompiling the 12KB jar's EventListener.class bytecode; ClientSimplyHarvesting.class exists only for the (client-unsupported) mod-loading stub). Only one config option exists (ServerConfig.disableOnSneaking, ground-truthed via bytecode - no decompiler available, confirmed via the upstream ChaoticTrials/SimplyHarvesting GitHub source for the exact 1.21-3.0.3 tag: `define("disableOnSneaking", false)`), left at its shipped default (false) so right-click-harvest works the same whether or not the player happens to be sneaking - no config shipped. Excludes berry bushes by design (data/simplyharvesting/tags/block/ignore_harvesting.json ships #c:berry_bushes) since those already have their own correct partial-harvest vanilla behavior.

GitHub issue #69 item 3 (speed half): 'Look up to climb faster and look down to descend faster on climbable blocks' (verbatim from its own neoforge.mods.toml description) - a direct match for the issue's 'faster ladder climbing based on look direction' ask. Verified via version-metadata API and jar extraction: ZERO dependencies beyond neoforge/minecraft. Modrinth lists client_side=unsupported/server_side=required, but that's a metadata mismatch we caught by inspecting the jar directly - it ships a real FasterLadderClimbingNeoForgeClient class, a ClimbConfigScreen/ClimbSpeedSlider settings GUI, and a ClimbPolicyPayload network packet syncing the server's speed policy to the client, so side:both is the ground-truthed classification, not client_side=unsupported. Zero mixins (grep of the jar's own class list confirms no `mixin` package at all) - it hooks a plain NeoForge tick/interact event and calls Player.move()/setDeltaMovement() directly, so it cannot conflict with Epic Fight's mixin-based animation override (see epicfight_mode_sync.js, pack/kubejs/server_scripts/) - the two operate on different layers (physics vs. rendering) and were verified to touch none of the same classes. Ground-truthed config defaults via constant-pool disassembly of FasterLadderClimbingSettings.class/ServerClimbPolicy.class (no decompiler available): the mod's own DEFAULT_UP_SPEED/DEFAULT_DOWN_SPEED (0.2/-0.2 blocks-per-tick) exactly equal vanilla's own climb speed - i.e. out of the box, before any config, the mod changes nothing perceptible, because its speed boost is `player's configured speed minus vanilla's own baseline`. The shipped server policy (ServerClimbPolicy.DEFAULT: enabled=true, upSpeedLimit=1.25, downSpeedLimit=-1.25, the mod's own MAX_UP_SPEED/MAX_DOWN_SPEED clamp bounds) is already maximally permissive, so no server.json is shipped. pack/config/fasterladderclimbing/client.json ships this pack's own tuned default (climbUpSpeed 0.6 / climbDownSpeed -0.6, 3x vanilla, within the mod's [0.05,1.25]/[-1.25,-0.05] clamp range) so the QoL is actually felt by default rather than requiring every player to find and raise their own client-side slider first; climbMode is left at the mod's own default (INPUT_FIRST, not the stricter LOOK_ONLY) since normal forward-held ladder climbing already reads look pitch under that mode and INPUT_FIRST avoids fighting ordinary WASD movement, matching the mod author's own recommended default.

GitHub issue #69 item 4: dedicated inventory trash slot (drag an item in, it's deleted) plus delete keybinds. By far the most popular candidate for this ask (4.6M downloads/850 followers vs. Mobile Trashcan's 2.5K/4 and Trashapack's 1.4K/1) and needs no new dependency surface: its one required dep (Modrinth version-metadata) is Balm, already installed since phase 12 for clientsort. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=required -> side:both (matches the jar: it's a Balm-framework config-synced mod, not purely cosmetic). Config (TrashSlotConfigData, ground-truthed via bytecode disassembly of its <init> field-initializer instructions - no decompiler available): instantDeletion=false, enableDeleteKeysInCreative=true, enableHints=true, allowDeletionWhileTrashSlotIsInvisible=false, deletionDenyList=empty, all left at their real shipped defaults - instantDeletion=false in particular means dropped items still go through the normal short despawn window rather than vanishing the instant they touch the slot, a sensible accidental-delete safety margin to keep. deletionDenyList (comma-separated modid:name list) is deliberately left empty rather than pre-populated with this pack's boss-unique drops: same reasoning as the #7 creative_crate note elsewhere in this project - there is no existing registry of 'genuinely unique, must-never-trash' item ids to check against in this codebase, so guessing a list would be unfounded; left for a follow-up if an accidental-trash incident is ever reported.

GitHub issue #104 (FEATURE, approved): required dep for Controlling below (its own neoforge.mods.toml declares searchables [1.0.1,) REQUIRED, side=CLIENT). Verified via version-metadata API: searchables 1.0.2 itself has zero dependencies. Modrinth client_side=required/server_side=unsupported -> side:client. A small, standalone search/filter/autocomplete helper library with no functionality of its own beyond what depending mods (Controlling) surface.

GitHub issue #104 (FEATURE, approved): searchable keybinds - owner named this exact mod as the example to evaluate ('Controlling'). Adds a search box plus a 'show conflicts only' filter to the vanilla Controls screen, letting players filter by keybind name (which, per the mod's own description, covers filtering by the originating mod's action names and by key) - directly satisfies the ask. Chosen over the newer, purpose-built 'Keybind Search' mod (CC BY-NC-4.0 licensed - a non-commercial restriction unlike this pack's other picks - and orders of magnitude smaller adoption) given Controlling's 33M+ Modrinth downloads, MIT license, and active release history through this exact neoforge-1.21.1-19.0.5 build (2025-03-30). Ground-truthed via jar extraction of its own neoforge.mods.toml: requires neoforge [21.1.94,) (installed 21.1.235 satisfies), minecraft [1.21.1] exact, and searchables [1.0.1,) (added directly above) - all three deps declared side=CLIENT in its own toml, matching Modrinth's client_side=required/server_side=unsupported -> side:client classification. Lowest priority of this round's three issues (feature vs. #103/#107's bugs), added last. No config needed - purely an added UI affordance on the existing Controls screen; verify-in-game that the search box appears and filters correctly against this pack's large combined keybind list (Epic Fight, Create, etc.).

GitHub issue #107: required dep for gravestone-x-curios-api-compat below (its own neoforge.mods.toml declares baguettelib [1.0.0,) REQUIRED). Zero dependencies of its own (version-metadata API confirms an empty dependency list across all its neoforge 1.21.1 releases); pinned to the current latest (2.0.3, 2026-04-05) since the compat mod's own version range is wide open ([1.0.0,)). CORRECTED (client-test-harness hardening, 2026-07-23, same bug class as the epic-tweaks side miss): was side:server on Modrinth's client_side=unsupported/server_side=required metadata alone - wrong. javap decompilation of the actually-installed baguettelib-1.21.1-NeoForge-2.0.3.jar's Baguettelib constructor (which runs unconditionally on both dists, not gated on FMLEnvironment.dist) shows it always calls NetworkManager.create("baguettelib", modBus) and registers an ExamplePacket under channel "example" via .serverToClient() - leftover template/boilerplate code, not gated behind any actual feature check. NeoForge's client/server channel-version handshake then requires that exact channel on the client too; with baguettelib absent client-side, every connect attempt is rejected with "Incompatible client! Please use NeoForge 21.1.235" (L3's real-client-join tier caught this - Modrinth's client_side/server_side labels only describe the AUTHOR'S INTENDED usage, not what the jar's own code unconditionally does at runtime, and this is the second time that gap has bitten this pack). gravestone-x-curios-api-compat (below) has no network/channel code of its own (confirmed by class listing: only mixins + event handlers, no NetworkManager/registerPacket call) and its neoforge.mods.toml lists baguettelib as a plain required dependency with no side constraint, so it stays side:server unaffected - baguettelib now installed on both sides is a superset of before, nothing about the compat mod's own server-only logic needs to change.

GitHub issue #107 (BUG): Gravestone restores armour/items to their correct slots but not Curios. Ground-truthed against the actually-installed gravestone-neoforge-1.21.1-1.0.37.jar (phase 22): its own neoforge.mods.toml declares zero Curios dependency and jar extraction confirms zero 'curio'-named classes/resources anywhere in the 198-entry jar - henkelmax's Gravestone Mod has NO Curios integration whatsoever, config toggle or otherwise (ruling out option (a), a config fix - there is no such option to flip). This is option (b): a dedicated compat addon, 'Gravestone x Curios API Compat', exists specifically to close this gap ('allows you to directly equip your Curios items to the respective slots' per its own Modrinth description) - a mod swap (option (c)) is unnecessary and would be far more disruptive than this small, purpose-built addon. Pinned to 4.0.2 (2026-04-07) rather than the older 3.0.1/2.1.0 releases specifically BECAUSE its changelog directly targets this issue's exact complaint: 'Individual slot tracking ensuring curios restore to their original positions' (plus cosmetic-slot support, Curse of Binding handling, and Curios' own keepCurios-config compliance) - a full rewrite over the older versions' simpler restore logic. Ground-truthed via jar extraction of its own neoforge.mods.toml (note: internal package/class names read 'corpsecurioscompat' - a shared codebase with the author's sibling 'Corpse x Curios API Compat' mod for a different death-storage mod; cosmetic naming leftover, not a wrong-jar mixup, confirmed by its mixin targets below): requires gravestone [1.0.24,) (installed 1.0.37 satisfies), curios [9.0.0,) (installed 9.5.1 satisfies), baguettelib [1.0.0,) (added directly above), neoforge [4,) and minecraft [1.21.1,1.22) - all straightforward, no version conflicts. Its two mixins (GraveStoneBlockMixin, CurioStacksHandlerMixin, both required, JAVA_21 compatibility level) directly target henkelmax's actual GraveStoneBlock class and Curios' own CurioStacksHandler class, confirming a genuine, targeted integration rather than a generic reimplementation. Modrinth client_side=unsupported/server_side=required -> side:server (matches: Curios restoration on grave-break is server-authoritative inventory logic, same as Gravestone's own core mechanism). No config shipped - the mod's behavior (restore Curios to original slots on grave pickup) is its sole purpose with no documented toggle found in its class list; verify-in-game that breaking/sneak-picking-up a grave now returns worn Curios items to their original trinket slots instead of the general inventory.

GitHub #109 cutover: vppquests (mods-src/vppquests/, see its README.md and DESIGN.md's '#109' sections) is now the pack's SOLE quest system, following the mods-src/<modid>/ convention #67 established (vppintegration is the other worked example). Both sides required - QuestScreen's GUI and keybind are client-rendered, but QuestRegistry/QuestReloadListener/QuestProgressAttachment/QuestProgressTracker are server-authoritative. The owner reported in 0.5.2 that the old KubeJS quest tracker (quests.js, GitHub #33) plus its vanilla-advancement GUI bolt-on (#36/#66) was broken end to end (progress wasn't recognized at all) and directed a full cutover rather than more migration work on dead code: quests.js, its advancement-generation code (scripts/gen_quests.py), the 62 generated advancement JSON files, and their CI checks (check_quests.py/check_advancements.py) are all REMOVED (not deprecated-in-place); the legacy-progress migration path (QuestLegacyMigration) is also removed per the owner's explicit 'don't worry about porting existing quests/progress' - vppquests starts every player fresh, no carried-forward state. The 62-quest/10-chapter content itself (which wasn't what was broken) is kept, now generated straight into vppquests' own schema by scripts/gen_vppquests_data.py (self-contained - no longer imports the removed gen_quests.py) into pack/kubejs/data/vanillaplusplus/vppquests/{chapter,quest}/**, validated by the new scripts/ci/check_vppquests.py. achievements.js and dailies.js (separate features - lifetime achievements and daily bounties, both granting #120's unified 'adventurer' skill XP) were checked for any dependency on the removed quest infra and have none (both use their own progress-key/persistence helpers) - left completely untouched. No dependency requirements of its own (a standalone, Modrinth-publishable mod per its own README - zero required deps in its neoforge.mods.toml).
